title: eulophia coeloglossa
intro: Eulophia coeloglossa, also known as the "Lipstick Orchid" or "Coral Root Orchid," is a type of orchid native to tropical Africa, characterized by its vibrant red to orange flowers and green, waxy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized in various herbal preparations. The health benefits of Eulophia coeloglossa include its potential to treat skin conditions, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, although further research is necessary to confirm these effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are used in topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
